title: coordinator/teammate: Teammate derives Clone but TeammateId equality marks them as the same agent
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
solid
## Location
src/coordinator/teammate.rs:114-128 (Teammate struct)
## Violation
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Teammate {
    pub id: TeammateId,
    ...
}
```

`TeammateId` is the identity key used by `Coordinator::teammates: HashMap<TeammateId, Teammate>` (src/coordinator/mod.rs:45) and by every per-teammate cache including `LeaderPermissionBridge::always_allowed: HashSet<(TeammateId, String)>` (src/coordinator/permission.rs:48). The `derive(Clone)` on `Teammate` produces a second value that compares equal (same id) but has a wholly separate `TeammateState`, transcript path, and session id. Two clones can diverge: one transitions to Dead while the other is still Running, and the coordinator can't tell which is canonical.

The unique-identity invariant is contradicted by the derive: a teammate is either a singleton (no Clone) or a thin pointer (Arc).

## Mandated refactor
Drop `Clone` from `Teammate`. If callers need shared access, wrap in `Arc<RwLock<Teammate>>` or move the mutable state behind an `Arc<Mutex<TeammateState>>` so all readers see the same lifecycle row. Keep `Clone` only on the read-only fields (id, color, agent_type) if a snapshot is needed.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
